entries.title: Han and Leia Marriage Starts After Courtship

content.fact: Han and Leia’s marriage belongs after the Courtship/Dathomir/Hapes period, not immediately after Endor by default.
content.injection: Before the marriage anchor, write Han and Leia as committed but not yet married unless the user’s story changes it; after it, use spouse/family dynamics.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Han and Leia Married.
Respect story-position bounds from Han and Leia Married to Han and Leia Married.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

content.fact: The Solo children make Han and Leia’s public family life a recurring security, political, and emotional pressure point.
content.injection: After the children are born, include kidnapping risk, protection arrangements, public scrutiny, and parental strain when relevant.

entries.title: Luke and Leia Sibling Bond Has Political Sensitivity

context.label: Post-Endor New Republic before the New Jedi Order
context.phase: Early New Republic to New Jedi Order Threshold

content.fact: Luke and Leia’s sibling bond and Vader lineage can carry personal and political sensitivity in New Republic contexts.
content.injection: Use Luke and Leia’s relationship as both emotional support and political risk when Vader’s legacy or Force lineage is relevant. Use this as a background rule only; narrower active era/source cards should override it.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Post-Endor New Republic before the New Jedi Order.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.
antiLore: Do not let general model memory override the selected Legends story position.

entries.title: Mara and Luke Slow Burn Starts from Distrust

content.fact: Mara and Luke’s later bond grows from distrust, survival, mutual respect, and unresolved Imperial trauma.
content.injection: Before Hand of Thrawn, frame Mara/Luke with tension and hard-won respect rather than instant domestic romance.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Thrawn Campaign.
Respect story-position bounds from Thrawn Returns to Thrawn Defeated.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Leia Carries Vader Legacy as Public Burden

context.label: Post-Endor New Republic before the New Jedi Order
context.phase: Early New Republic to New Jedi Order Threshold

content.fact: Leia’s biological link to Vader is personally painful and politically exploitable in New Republic society.
content.injection: When Vader lineage is in play, let Leia’s public role make the secret or revelation more politically loaded than Luke’s private Jedi identity. Use this as a background rule only; narrower active era/source cards should override it.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Post-Endor New Republic before the New Jedi Order.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.

entries.title: Children Do Not Erase Leia’s Agency

context.label: Post-Endor New Republic before the New Jedi Order
context.phase: Early New Republic to New Jedi Order Threshold

content.fact: Leia’s motherhood adds obligations and vulnerabilities but does not remove her agency as diplomat, leader, and Force-sensitive Skywalker.
content.injection: Do not write post-childbirth Leia as sidelined by default; balance family stakes with political and personal agency. Use this as a background rule only; narrower active era/source cards should override it.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Post-Endor New Republic before the New Jedi Order.

entries.title: Han and Luke Friendship Survives New Roles

context.label: Post-Endor New Republic before the New Jedi Order
context.phase: Early New Republic to New Jedi Order Threshold

content.fact: Han and Luke’s friendship adapts as Luke becomes Jedi restorer and Han becomes husband/father/state actor.
content.injection: In New Republic scenes, keep Han and Luke’s trust and banter, but allow their obligations to pull them into different worlds. Use this as a background rule only; narrower active era/source cards should override it.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Post-Endor New Republic before the New Jedi Order.
